name: gmail-blade-mcp
title: Google Gmail
description: Gmail email operations via Google API — search, read, send, threads, labels, drafts, filters. Gemini-powered classification and summarisation.
author: groupthink-dev

highlights.text: Search & threads — Gmail query syntax, thread grouping, label filtering
Send & draft — compose, reply, forward, save drafts for review
Labels & filters — organise mail with labels and automated filter rules
Gemini AI — on-device classification and summarisation via Gemini Flash
OAuth 2.0 — standard Google consent flow, no stored passwords
Write-gated — reads always work, sends require explicit opt-in

readme:
### What it does

Gmail Blade gives your AI agent full access to a Gmail account through the official Google API. Tools cover search, reading, sending, thread management, labels, drafts, and filters. An optional Gemini integration adds on-device email classification and summarisation using Google's lightweight Gemini 2.0 Flash Lite model.

Your agent can search by Gmail query syntax, read full message content, manage labels, create draft replies for human review, and set up filter rules — all through a clean tool interface.

### Gemini integration

When a `GOOGLE_API_KEY` is provided, Gmail Blade can classify incoming messages by priority and category, and generate concise summaries of long threads. This runs via the Gemini API — not the host LLM — so it works regardless of which model is driving the agent. The Gemini features are optional; all core email operations work without an API key.

### Safety model

All tools follow a read-free, write-gated pattern:

- **Reads** work immediately — search, read messages, list labels, view threads
- **Writes** require explicit opt-in — send, reply, create drafts, modify labels, create filters

### Setup

Gmail Blade uses OAuth 2.0 with the standard Google consent flow. You create a Cloud project, enable the Gmail API, download OAuth credentials, and complete a one-time browser-based authorisation. No passwords are stored — the refresh token handles subsequent sessions.

tools.name: gmail_search
tools.description: Search messages by Gmail query language (from:, to:, subject:, has:, newer_than:, etc.). Server-side filter with DD-278 scope-tag wrapper (scope=work|personal|family|public). Stable ordering by internalDate desc, id asc.

tools.name: gmail_read
tools.description: Read a single message by ID — headers, body, attachments metadata. DD-278 scope-tag wrapper (scope=work|personal|family|public) verifies labelIds post-fetch.

tools.name: gmail_snippets
tools.description: Compact snippet view across recent or filtered messages. DD-278 scope-tag wrapper (scope=work|personal|family|public). Stable ordering by internalDate desc, id asc.

tools.name: gmail_thread
tools.description: Read an entire thread by thread ID with all messages. DD-278 scope-tag wrapper (scope=work|personal|family|public) verifies labelIds post-fetch.

tools.name: gmail_mailboxes
tools.description: List all labels (mailboxes) in the account. Stable ordering by name asc (case-folded), id asc.

tools.name: gmail_info
tools.description: Account health — quota, identity, label count, write gate status.

tools.name: gmail_state
tools.description: Current sync state and account-wide counters.

tools.name: gmail_changes
tools.description: Incremental changes since a history ID.

tools.name: gmail_identities
tools.description: Send-as identities and aliases configured in the account. Stable ordering by sendAsEmail asc (case-folded).

tools.name: gmail_filters
tools.description: Server-side filter rules configured in the account. Stable ordering by id asc.

tools.name: gmail_send
tools.description: Send a new message. Write — gated by GMAIL_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: gmail_reply
tools.description: Reply to a thread. Write — gated by GMAIL_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: gmail_draft
tools.description: Create a draft message. Write — gated by GMAIL_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: gmail_flag
tools.description: Add or remove labels on messages. Reversible write — gated.

tools.name: gmail_move
tools.description: Move messages between labels. Reversible write — gated.

tools.name: gmail_bulk
tools.description: Bulk operations on a set of message IDs. Write — gated.

tools.name: gmail_delete
tools.description: Permanently delete messages. Destructive — gated by GMAIL_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: gmail_filter_create
tools.description: Create a server-side filter rule. Write — gated.

tools.name: gmail_filter_delete
tools.description: Delete a server-side filter rule. Destructive — gated.

tools.name: gmail_classify
tools.description: Classify a message against pre-configured categories. Read-only inference.

tools.name: gmail_summarise
tools.description: Generate a token-efficient summary of a message or thread.

env.name: GOOGLE_API_KEY
env.description: Google API key for Gemini AI features (optional — classification and summarisation)

env.name: GMAIL_WORK_LABEL
env.description: Gmail q= fragment for DD-278 scope=work (default: category:work). Override e.g. label:Work for custom workflows.

env.name: GMAIL_PERSONAL_LABEL
env.description: Gmail q= fragment for DD-278 scope=personal (default: category:personal).

env.name: GMAIL_FAMILY_LABEL
env.description: Gmail q= fragment for DD-278 scope=family (default: label:Family).

setup.help_text: 1. Create a project at Google Cloud Console. 2. Enable the Gmail API. 3. Create OAuth 2.0 credentials (Desktop app type). 4. Download the JSON and save as ~/.gmail-blade/credentials.json. 5. On first run, complete the OAuth consent flow in your browser.
prerequisites: Google Cloud project with Gmail API enabled
setup.blurb: Gmail email operations via Google API — search, read, send, threads, labels, drafts, filters. Gemini-powered classification and summarisation.

scenarios.label: Classify and triage unread mail
scenarios.body: Search for unread messages, use Gemini classification to sort by priority, label high-priority items for immediate attention, and archive low-priority newsletters.

scenarios.label: Draft replies for a thread
scenarios.body: Read a multi-message thread, generate a context-aware draft reply, and save it for human review before sending.

scenarios.label: Set up inbox automation with filters
scenarios.body: Analyse recent mail patterns, create filter rules to auto-label recurring senders, and verify the filters are working on new incoming messages.
